name: Shortest Distance in a Binary Maze
statement: Given an n x m matrix grid where each cell contains either 0 or 1, determine the shortest distance between a source cell and a destination cell. You can move to an adjacent cell (up, down, left, or right) if that adjacent cell has a value of 1. The path can only be created out of cells containing 1. If the destination cell is not reachable from the source cell, return -1.
constraints:
• 1 ≤ n, m ≤ 500
  • grid[i][j] == 0 or grid[i][j] == 1
  • The source and destination cells are always inside the given matrix.

examples.explanation: The shortest path from (0, 1) to (2, 2) is: Move down to (1, 1) Move down to (2, 1) Move right to (2, 2) Thus, the shortest distance is 3
Since, there is no path possible between the source cell and the destination cell, hence we return -1.
